title: Tender Velvety Kenyan Seed Dumplings
description: A sour and velvety Kenyan dish featuring seeds and dumplings, perfect for a crowd, and vegetarian friendly. This recipe is a unique and delicious twist on traditional Kenyan cuisine.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the sorghum flour and salt.
Add the pumpkin seeds, sunflower seeds, and vegetable oil to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Gradually add the water to the bowl, stirring with a wooden spoon until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it is smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a golf ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and set aside.
Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium heat and add a few of the dumplings to the skillet.
Cook the dumplings for 3-4 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and cooked through.
Serve the dumplings hot with a squeeze of lemon juice and a sprinkle of chopped parsley.
